result:
1. Rate limiter `multi.exec()` result destructured wrong — `src/edge/rate_limit.ts` §4 snippet: `const [count] = await multi.exec();`. ioredis `exec()` returns `[[err, incrResult], [err, expireResult]]`, so `count === [null, N]`; `[null,N] > limit` is always false. Rate limiting silently disabled. Fix: `const res = await multi.exec(); const count = res[0][1];` (or use `.incr` + `.expire` separately and read the INCR reply).
2. `X-Internal-*` headers absent from §2 Rule 1 strip list — Rule 1 enumerates `X-User-Id`, `X-Org-Id`, `X-Forwarded-For`, `X-Real-IP`, `X-Original-Authorization`; §5 introduces `X-Internal-User-Id`/`X-Internal-Org-Id`/`X-Internal-Roles`/`X-Internal-Auth-Method` but never tells `header_sanitize` to strip them. Attacker injects `X-Internal-User-Id: <victim>`; if sanitize follows doc, downstream mTLS consumer trusts it = impersonation. Fix: add `X-Internal-*` to Rule 1 and assert in `header_sanitize.test.ts`.
3. Auto-rollback on SEV1 contradicts security forensics directive — §6 says any SEV1 auto-rolls back per region; §8 says "DO NOT roll back without coordination" for security alerts. First security SEV1 destroys forensic state and may reintroduce vulnerable build. Fix: exclude `security-*` alert classes from auto-rollback trigger; require security-oncall ack.
4. Rate-limit key missing region prefix in §4 sample — 2025-12 SEV2 RCA added region prefix but code block still reads `rl:{ip}:{route_prefix}:{minute_bucket}`. Engineers copy-paste from handbook, reintroduce the global-limit regression. Fix: update §4 to `rl:{region}:{ip}:{route_prefix}:{minute_bucket}` and add a regression test that fails if the key lacks the region segment.
5. Fixed-window burst at bucket boundary — `minute_bucket = Math.floor(Date.now()/60000)` gives hard 60s windows, allowing ~2× limit in ~1s across the boundary; §4 advertises "sliding-window approximation". Cheap amplification for brute-force/scraping. Fix: switch to two-bucket rolling sum or GCRA; at minimum document the burst.
6. JWT `exp` grace accepts 30s-expired tokens plus 30s revoked-API-key cache — §5 rejects only when `exp < Date.now() - skewMs` (30s) and §5 LRU caches API key lookups 30s. Stolen/rotated credential remains live ~30s after revocation on each path. Fix: drop skew to 5s on `exp` (clock drift between your own services is sub-second), add explicit revocation bus to bust the LRU.
7. `Retry-After` HTTP header not set on 429 — §4 returns JSON `retry_after` field only; RFC 6585 clients (browsers, many SDKs) read the `Retry-After` response header. Legitimate clients hammer instead of backing off, distorting SLO + wasting capacity. Fix: `res.set('Retry-After', secondsUntilNextBucket())` alongside the JSON body.
8. CODEOWNERS approval gate omits error/logger/body-guard — §6 requires human approval on `auth_jwt.ts`, `rate_limit.ts`, `header_sanitize.ts`, `trusted_proxies.json`. `src/edge/errorHandler.ts` (stacktrace/IP leak per §2 Rule 6), `src/util/logger.ts` (PII redaction per §2 Rule 5), `src/edge/body_size_guard.ts` (DoS guard) are unreviewed. Fix: add these paths to CODEOWNERS security block.
9. PII redaction list vs "never log" list diverge — §2 Rule 5 redaction covers `email|phone|tax_id|card_pan|passport`; §11 forbids logging `Authorization|Cookie|X-Api-Key` but nothing enforces it. If a dev logs `req.headers`, the pii middleware won't scrub auth material — discipline-only control. Also missing: refresh tokens, OAuth `code`/`state`, session IDs, JWT `jti`. Fix: extend `pii_redact` to header-key denylist and add test that logs `req` shape to verify scrubbing.
10. `request_id = OTel trace ID` returned in error body — §2 Rule 6 mandates trace-id exposure to clients. OTel trace IDs are 128-bit random (not sensitive), but returning them lets a client enumerate Tempo/Grafana if any internal dashboard is ever misconfigured public, and couples external contract to internal telemetry ID format. Fix: emit a separate public `request_id` and map it to trace-id server-side in the audit log.
